titel: Freedom from violence, oppression, and exploitation: A national strategy to combat violence against women by men, domestic violence, exploitation in prostitution and human trafficking, and honor-related violence and oppression.
html:
In this document, the government presents a new national strategy for gender equality to combat violence, oppression, and exploitation, covering the period 2026–2035. The purpose of the strategy is to contribute to effective, sustainable, and coordinated efforts to address violence against women by men, domestic violence, exploitation in prostitution and human trafficking, and honor-related violence and oppression.

The government also explains in detail the meaning of the sixth gender equality policy objective, which aims to eliminate violence against women by men, domestic violence, and exploitation in prostitution and human trafficking, as well as the seventh gender equality policy objective, which aims to eliminate honor-related violence and oppression.

The document also includes an overview of the work to prevent and combat violence against women for the period 2017–2025.
tldr: The government has developed a new plan to combat violence and oppression in Sweden by the year 2035. The plan aims to help authorities work together more effectively to address various forms of violence against women and honor-based abuse.
lattlast:
The government is now presenting a new national strategy to be implemented between the years 2026 and 2035. The goal of the strategy is to create a strong and coordinated effort to stop violence in close relationships, human trafficking, and exploitation in prostitution. It also aims to stop honor-related violence, which involves oppression based on family or group.

In the document, the government explains what they mean by their goals for gender equality. Gender equality means that men and women should have the same rights and opportunities. The government outlines exactly what is required to completely eliminate violence against women and honor-related oppression.

The text also includes a summary of what has been done previously. It examines how the work against violence against women has looked during the years between 2017 and 2025. This is done to understand what has worked and what needs to be improved in the future.
